text:
Apartment: 42N
Review: We found 42N while touring apt complexes during my daughter’s Freshman year. We instantly fell in love with it. The biggest thing going for it is all the natural green spaces and trees, not a concrete jungle many of the other complexes. It has a hammock garden, large pool and clubhouse, basketball courts, and is even pet friendly with a dog park area. There is plenty of parking and we’ve never had an issue finding a spot. Is close enough to campus without being right next to it, and has a bull runner stop directly in front of the complex. The inside of the units are spacious and each unit has an outdoor patio. It is clean, safe, and very affordable in comparison to other complexes we saw. It is not brand new but we are totally fine with that. The office staff and management of the complex are always very nice and as well.

Apartment: 42N
Review: We have had to contact management a couple of times with minor issues, and our issues have always been resolved quickly. My daughter has lived there a full year and will be living there again!

Apartment: 42N
Review: To the Management Team at 42N, I wanted to take a few moments to express my sincere gratitude to your entire team for the outstanding service and kindness we experienced when my daughter recently moved into your community. When we first arrived in Tampa on January 6, our initial plan was for her to move into another property near USF. Unfortunately, that situation turned out to be unsafe and unacceptable, leaving us feeling worried and uncertain. We began searching for a new place right away and found 42N based on glowing Google reviews. From the moment we walked in, we were greeted by four smiling and welcoming staff members - each one making us feel seen, supported, and cared for in a stressful situation. A very special thank you goes to Lucy Gates and Pricilla, who were incredibly kind, compassionate, and understanding from the start.

Apartment: 42N
Review: After hearing what had happened, she and the rest of your amazing team - Priscilla, Hayden, Christian, Marina, and DaiAnera - worked tirelessly to help us secure an apartment. Even though nothing was supposed to be available until January 19, they were able to make it happen within just two days, and my daughter was moved in by January 8. I know that required extra effort and coordination behind the scenes, and their willingness to go above and beyond will always mean so much to us. I would also to commend your maintenance team - Hugo, Eddie, and Scott - for their exceptional service. They responded immediately to requests on move-in day, took care of a long list of items efficiently and cheerfully, and left everything perfectly done. Their professionalism and positive attitude truly stood out.

Apartment: 42N
Review: Please make sure each of these staff members is recognized for their outstanding performance and dedication. They turned what started as a stressful and uncertain experience into a positive and uplifting one. We are so thankful to be part of a community where the people genuinely care about their residents. With heartfelt appreciation.

Apartment: 42N
Review: This place is advertised as no smoking but very much has many smokers. I wish they would actually enforce it as an asthmatic. I've seen staff smoke IN my apartment and in other apartments, so I have little faith anything will be done about this. If your health is impacted by smoke as mine is, I advise you stay elsewhere.

Apartment: 42N
Review: While my son, fiance and I were using the hot tub at 42 North Apartments, we were approached by a security officer with dark brown hair and a thick beard. His tone was very rude as he instructed us to leave the hot tub. We complied and went to the bathroom to change clothes. During this time, the officer remained positioned outside the bathroom door, waiting for us to exit. Once we returned to my vehicle, the officer walked behind my car and took photos of my license plate. His actions made us feel uncomfortable and as though we were being followed or surveilled without cause. The following morning, my son called the leasing office to report the incident. Residents should not be subjected to being followed or stalked by security staff, and I feel this behavior was inappropriate and unprofessional.

Apartment: 42N
Review: I'd to get my son out of this area and in to a place where he can enjoy his free time without being targeted by his race or skin color. Neighbors at 42 North. The towing company towed several cars while we were in the hot tub. It was sad to watch so many vehicles get towed. The security assisted with the towing. They walked the parking lot with flashlights. DON'T BACK YOUR VEHICLE IN THE PARKING SPACES. THEY TOW YOU. IT SHOULDN'T MATTER HOW YOU PARK YOUR VEHICLE WHEN YOU PAY RENT.

Apartment: 42N
Review: DO NOT LIVE HERE!!!!! If I could put zero stars I would. So I was being bullied by my roommates and requested to be moved out of my unit and they expect me to pay $300 to move to a different unit when I am the one being bullied and harassed by my roommates and forced out of my room. Horrible management, they do not deal with issues at all they kind of just brush you off. Second, my unit was a complete biohazard when I moved in. They did not clean my unit at all before I moved in. They also have a very big roach and rat problem. Packages get stolen all the time too. DO NOT LIVE HERE. They also allow grown adults that are above 30 to live with 18 or 20 year old students which is completely unsafe because these people obviously bring home people (men) who are their age into an apartment with young college girls.

Apartment: 42N
Review: would not recommend signing a lease here under the current management. Despite months of complaints about my door being left unlocked by staff, the issue persists—most recently within the last two weeks. Management has repeatedly dismissed my concerns, at times denying them despite video evidence, and only responded after I issued a formal Notice to Cure. I have also experienced unprofessional conduct from staff, including dismissive remarks and facial expressions when addressing safety concerns. This is especially troubling given recent incidents in the complex, such as a neighbor’s car being broken into. While the property itself is average, the lack of responsiveness and disregard for resident safety make it unacceptable.

Apartment: 42N
Review: 42N is the perfect community for USF students! From its close to campus location to its modern apartments and amenities, the property embodies student living! The kind and responsive management and staff also elevate the experience! Lucy in the front office is the absolute sweetest and has helped me navigate my time at 42N :)

Apartment: 42N
Review: Went in a tour today and I loved what I saw. I was very surprised I d it so much. Very spacious rooms, bathrooms, and dining areas. Good Guest parking is is a must for me and they have it!

Apartment: 42N
Review: I was visiting my friend who lives at 42N Apartments and decided to hang out at the swimming area to relax. Out of nowhere, a security guard which I believe it’s Signal of Tampa company walked straight up to me and started asking if I lived there, what my friend’s address was, and other personal questions. I explained I was there as a guest, but instead of being understanding, he told me to leave. His tone and attitude were unreasonably rude, making me feel unwelcome and uncomfortable. After that experience, I have no interest in ever visiting again or using any of their amenities.

Apartment: 42N
Review: My son is in his second year of college and decided to move to another off campus student housing complex after repeated problems at Union on Fletcher. Although there was a hiccup upon move in, the manager Dae validated my concerns and took the appropriate steps to rectify our experience. She was super kind and professional. This weekend 42nd N has grown on me as a apartment “home” type complex that is tree lined, peaceful and importantly close to campus. Hoping my son will feel happy here the next several years. 🙏🏼

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: It has been already three times in just a year that the water went out. An ESSENTIAL need. It is inexcusable for this to keep happening and all they can say is “sorry for the inconvenience”??? It’s they think we have nothing to do in our lives. They are horrible and never respect or take care of your essential needs. And there is much disrespectful things they do; they never clean the pool, delay to fix the elevator, have people enter your room without permission???, my roomate even had her food stolen by a repairman. Even if this place is close to USF it will be a wayyyy much inconvenience than just living somewhere farther away, it’s not worth living here.

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: The outside of the building doesnt look the best, but it looks pretty alright from the inside. Its spacious, furnished, and pretty cheap all things considered. Also, the receptionists at the front desk are very . About , Paris was really welcoming and gave me a good tour of the place which convinced me to sign a lease, and both her and Jade have been super friendly at the front desk. Orisha If there’s one review you should trust, it’s this one. I graduated from USF four months ago, and I still hate this apartment to my core. Absolutely horrible experience. …

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: Avoid this place at all costs. The leasing office staff is incompetent and will do nothing to help—just make excuses to defend management, who are never in the office. The living conditions here are almost getting to the point where its inhabitable. The elevator has been broken for 6-7 months, trash piles up, pool and gym gates are broken, and there are no keypads on entry gates, making deliveries impossible. Meanwhile, they find every excuse to charge residents while failing to maintain basic living conditions. Recent good reviews are fake—left by people bribed with gift cards during tours. Save yourself the headache and look elsewhere there are many better and cheaper options available around USF. just an example of how bad the situation is, these are all different trash pile ups. 5

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: I’ve had a terrible experience living in this apartment community. First there was an issue with 2 of my roommates where they had outside individuals not on the lease staying in the apartment. When I made it known what was going on with evidence they told me the only thing they told me they could do was move me out of the unit I was in, into another one. This issue was taking place for 3 Months before they offered the move out option. Then when I did end up moving into a different unit they double charged me on my rent and me paying 300 dollars than what I was suppose to pay. This is a terrible place to live and I would not recommend living here!!!!

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: When we first moved in it was dirty. There is hair stuck to the floor and baseboards and the windows are moldy. There are roaches everywhere. They currently have 1 maintenance person and no maintenance manager. During the beginning of fall semester we had no AC for 3 weeks. The manager ignored our emails and there is no emergency maintenance. Most of the front desk people are mean and do not care about your problem. My roommates shower has been broken for 3 months and they said they would send someone by and never did. The gates never work and parking isn’t great. Amenities suck and half of their gym equipment doesn’t work. DO NOT LIVE HERE. 5

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: Lived here for a few months now and I really it. The leasing office is super friendly and they always have fun activities going on. They even brought snacks and energy drinks directly to our doors during finals. The personal balcony is a really nice touch that lets my room feel a lot private and personalized.An entire lot is dedicated to visitors and there's always parking available. It's been really convenient to be so close to everything. The Bull Market, Publix, USf, and food trucks are all available within walking distance. 1

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: The broken elevator is a serious problem .. with the time of the year where students move in / out, it needs to be fixed asap. Living on the 3rd floor is no joke carrying boxes up and down the stairways in 100 degree heat. It’s a dangerous situation which we can’t see the stair as we carrying big heavy boxes. We called the front office and the corporate office also, it looks won’t be fix anytime soon. For those who wants to live in Avalon please read all these reviews first.

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: Incredibly mediocre(especially for the price). The worst was roaches for the last week, the management sent someone but it just wasn’t enough. I never had i issues with mold. The dryer was kind of bad I was in building C.

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: It’s my first year living at avalon and overall my experience with living here has been great! Staff (specifically Yve) have always been nice and with any situations I have had. Maintenance for me personally has been overall great and speedy. However unfortunately I did have a small mold situation which did get solved fast.

Apartment: Avalon heights
Review: Do not sign a lease here. It’s overpriced for the living conditions they provide. The entire time I was there, it smelled rat feces coming out of my unit, we had a fly/maggot infestation due to their lousy trash valet service. You are better off throwing it out on your own. Maintenance would sometimes mark they came by when no one checked to resolve the issue. I had to leave earlier than expected due to a medical condition and the stairs were unsafe to go down, all the lights didn’t work any. Just a terrible first place to live on your own. If I could give 0/5 I would.

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: I want to give a big thank you to Liusmel and Tony at Cambridge Woods Apartments. They are truly great at what they do. I had an issue with my washer, and they responded super quickly and made the effort to fix it right away. When they realized the problem couldn’t be repaired, they didn’t just leave it there, they spoke with their supervisors and made sure a brand new washer was brought to my apartment in less than a week. They were professional and . It’s really reassuring to have maintenance staff who actually care about residents and follow through. Really appreciate their help!

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: Anthony Pabellón is always incredibly and kind. Every time I’ve needed maintenance, he shows up with great energy and a genuine willingness to help. Even when I don’t fully understand what’s going on or why something is happening, he takes the time to patiently explain everything in a way that’s easy to follow. On top of that, he always does a great job fixing the issue. It’s really reassuring to know there’s someone so reliable and positive taking care of the complex.

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: As a new resident I will say that Cambridge woods and Staff Viktoria Stojkov has made moving in a great smooth process. They respond to every email I send out in minutes, they explain and answer all my questions, the manager and maintenance also stopped by on move in day to make sure we had everything we needed on our first day. Which no apartment complex I’ve ever been to, goes this above and beyond to make sure their residents are taken care of. Thank you !

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: So far, I love living here. Every time I have a question, I'm able to go to the office and Viktoria is always there with a smile on her face, waiting to give assistance. I asked her a question about the internet, to which she gave me the appropriate numbers to call to have it taken care of. But even without me making the phone call, a week later, because she mentioned it to them, the internet company was out to test the system and fix the issue. The gym has everything I need and maintenance is always making sure things are taken care of.

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: This is the best apartment community I have ever lived in. The entire staff, especially Viktoria from the leasing office, was always attentive to my concerns. They ensured that all appliances and equipment were in working order before I moved in. The maintenance team consistently completed their work promptly, and the management treated me with the same respect during my final days as they did when I first arrived. This is the only apartment community that truly made me feel at home. P.S. Shoutout once again for Viktoria - the best leasing staff I have ever worked with!

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: Viktoria is the best manager I have ever seen in my 35 years of life. We only chose the place because of her, she made me feel Home away from home! I hope wish everyone in the industry was at least 10% as good as Viktoria! If you you are looking to rent a place and feel Home, work with Viktoria!

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: I love the townhouses! Cambridge woods is comfortable and safe. The staff is . Maintenance is great and always quick service. Scott Bilesimo is a very hard worker, super professional and quick to get the job done! *Daniel and Veronika are great in the office, always professional and if there are any concerns they are always quick to assist. Thank you all!

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: Scott is hands down one of the best maintenance professionals I’ve come across. Every time there’s an issue in the apartment, he shows up promptly and gets it fixed efficiently—no matter how big or small the problem is. What really sets him apart is that he doesn’t just fix things; he takes the time to explain exactly what went wrong and gives tips on how to avoid similar issues in the future. His attention to detail, professionalism, and willingness to educate make a big difference.

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: I’ve been living in this community for 3 years now, and it’s been a great experience throughout. I want to especially thank Victoria, who helped me find the perfect home and made the move-in process smooth and peaceful. Also, a big thank you to Scott for always responding to maintenance requests promptly and ensuring everything is taken care of quickly. Really appreciate the professionalism and support from the team!

Apartment: Cambridge Woods
Review: At first I was skeptical about the apartments. But I’m glad I moved in here. From the first day applying to being a whole year. The staff Daniel and Victoria has been phenomenal. When I put in work orders they see to maintenance getting the job done. I love the clubhouse and pool area. I rented it out for my daughter senior graduation party. My neighbors are kept to their selves and respectful

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Great apartments with everything you would need. Especially for the price/closeness to campus. As all d living spaces have there are some issues from time to time, but they've been responsive and always gave a path towards resolution. I've specifically been helped by Sara a few times for a some time sensitive issues. All in all, I definitely recommend checking out these apartments

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Issy was fantastic throughout my visit! She gave a thoughtful tour, answered all of my leasing questions clearly, and made the whole process feel easy and welcoming. Every time I stopped by the front desk while she was working, she took the time to help and made sure I left feeling comfortable and confident.

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: The team at Halo 46 is absolutely incredible! All of my needs were met while living at this complex. I want to sincerely thank Alex for helping me throughout my period of apartment hunting, she was a huge help and encouraged me throughout the process. She was able to provide me with great information regarding the complex and left me prepared to make a decision on where I wanted to live!! 1

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Along with the cleanliness and the amenities that are offered here, one of the best aspects about Halo 46 is their maintenance team. They are quick to answer and are so reliable. Met Eddie and Steve the day I moved in and they are some of the nicest people you will ever meet. They helped me that day and have helped with whatever I needed ever since!

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Living at Halo 46 has been wonderful during my time at college. I love going to the pool & using the gym! Also, Isabella at the front desk is always incredibly friendly and willing to help when I have questions. I highly recommend Halo!

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: My niece is a resident at Halo 46 apartment complex. She is happy to be a resident there and her experience with the Halo 46 staff has been nothing short of amazing! Anytime she has a concern it is addressed immediately. The front desk assistant Isabella Miranda always greets her with a smile and provides exceptional customer service. She goes above and beyond to address the needs of the residents.

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Toured a 2x2, Liz showed us around with a wonderful attitude and enthusiasm. She was very receptive to all concerns and answered questions quickly with definitive answers. Very kindly went over costs and leasing concerns with us. Great energy!

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: I’ve had a great experience living at Halo 46. The apartments are clean, modern, and fully furnished, which made moving in super easy. The pool and gym are always well kept, and maintenance responds quickly whenever needed. The location near USF is a huge plus too. Definitely recommend for students looking for convenience and comfort.

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: This place was amazing. Issy made sure to give me a thoughtful tour and answered all questions I had about leasing. Every time I came to the front desk and Issy was working, she ensured I left happily and without doubts that issues would be resolved!

Apartment: Halo 46
Review: Property maintenance workers Eddie and Steve are amazing!! Always and very attentive. They have always treated my roommates and I their own children, not only doing what needs to get done but leaving us with future advice.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Took a tour of the apartment today. The tour was good and the place looked nice. But the tour guide was extremely rude to me and implied that I had a fake ID when I handed it to him and implied it wasn’t my id 4 separate times. And he ruined my experience with the tour. I wouldn’t recommend living here if that’s the experience I first had when walking in.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: I want to write an honest review to help future college students who are looking for a reliable place to live. Larkon 42nd is not that place. There are better options on the same street with far better experiences. The biggest issue is management. They care about one thing only: money. They ignore resident concerns, do the bare minimum, and are not friendly or . Besides the maintenance staff who try their best, management and front desk office seems they have no clue about what goes on. Everything feels a fee. Parking? Extra. Power and water? Split between the whole building. You are getting charged for all that water and power your neighbours are using. And the fees….for example, we were charged $100 for a “door stain,” with no opportunity to wipe it off, on top of other random charges that made no sense.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: I've been living at Lark on 42nd, previously known as Uncommon apartments for 2.5 years. This is the best apartment complex for USF students. The staff has been so so amazing, nothing but love for this home. It's a little higher on price but we get what we pay! I'm very sad to be leaving. Make this your home while attending USF - no doubt!

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Editing my review because I transferred units and had a much better experience. I personally really looks of the apartment and think it's very well maintained. Sure, the hallways will sometimes be dirty but that's on the residents. It is mostly a college apt at the end of the day. I have seen the hallways be cleaned multiple times, they constantly repaint the garage, the elevator floors were recently replaced, and the exterior of the building was pressure washed too.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Do not live here. Overall I have had little problems with my actual unit but the water pressure is terrible. This is a building wide issue not just specific units. Also up until this point I have never had a bug problem, but my room is having some sort of wasp infestation. I’m not sure where they’re entering from but the abundance suggests there is a nest in the walls. Do not live here, it is not worth it. I’d save this place as a last resort. The halls are quite gross and always smell urine and the doors leading to the garage are not sealed, allowing bugs to easily get in.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Lark is okay at best—but if you’re looking for one of the cheapest furnished 2x2 apartments near USF with fewer roommates, it’s ly your best bet. While they overcharge for utilities ( than anywhere else I’ve lived), the total cost still comes out to less than most nearby options, even with utilities included.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: I don’t recommend this place to anyone. Dirty hallways and stairs, inhospitable staff(except security) will welcome you. Imagine the elevator of parking was not working for a YEAR AND A HALF! And worst of all, they are charging you each month at least extra 90 dollars for nothing. During winter break our apartment was empty( all my friends were on vacation), we paid 100 dollars extra each for unutilized electricity, water and savage. If you want to find out what is the reason, reception will send you off rudely. Please consider these facts, if you want to sign here.

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Lark on 42nd is the greatest option to live for a student at USF! It has great rates and is walking distance to the campus. The study area works perfectly for a study session. Roommate grouping worked for the best for me since now we are all best friends

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: Lark on 42nd has the best environment! The pool area is perfect for a weekend when you want to relax or if you want to get homework done during the week while being outside! The maintenance people is really nice. Sould recommend !

Apartment: Lark on 42nd
Review: I really enjoy living here at Lark, its sad to see all of these 1 star reviews over things that aren't the managements fault. I would recommend to those who are looking for a good apartment complex with easy campus access. Also shoutout to the whole front office for being so kind all the time, it goes a long way.

Apartment: On 50
Review: Stay away from this place. I had sewage coming out of my bathtub all year long, and they never fixed it. In fact, the property manager told me this is common in many first- and second-floor apartments and that they are not able to fix it. Further, they informed me that it’s mostly my own waste... I guess I should feel better!? AND no matter how much you clean their sewage-infested room, they will still charge you for cleaning at the end of your lease.

Apartment: On 50
Review: I just got here two days ago and I’m already loving it here. One person that is already got my heart the most is Mr. Justice a.k.a. J. I was having such a hectic day getting to Tampa let alone getting all my things in my moving truck and getting it to on 50 Apts. It was 6:45 when I called him and he literally stayed after hours for me and made sure that I securely got into my apartment and was able to rest and be assured that my stay here is going to be way better than the other places that I have stayed in a while !

Apartment: On 50
Review: My daughter lived in two different apartments and is still living there. Yes, some apartments are better than others. But both of hers have been ok. The location is great with school so close. The office staff is . She's had issues and they were taken care of promptly. I'm glad she lives at On50, for now.

Apartment: On 50
Review: ON50 might not be the most luxurious place ever, but it’s been a good home for me. The location is really nice, feels safe, and is near a lot of things. The office staff are always super kind and quick to help with anything. Maintenance is great too—really responsive, which I’ve found is pretty rare in apartments. Overall, I’ve had a really good experience living here the last four months.

Apartment: On 50
Review: I really love my apartment here. Ill tell you the good things first. The location is literally directly across the street from USF. Second amazing thing is how big/spacious the apartments are amd the beautiful preserve in the complex. Lastly but definitely not least are the amazing workers here. The maintenance team is super fast and the front desk do what thwy can to help. The cons: It is a bit older of an apartment so some furniture, appliances, and cabinets are bit worn. Overall, for the price and location I think this a solid place.

Apartment: On 50
Review: Staff and maintenance team are super nice and ! Apartments are clean, property is well maintained. Lots of community activities and opportunities to have fun! 10/10 experience. I’ve been living here for almost 4 years.

Apartment: On 50
Review: I strongly believe this place manipulates its reviews. Every time someone leaves negative feedback, a wave of new positive reviews appears to push the rating back up, it’s pretty obvious if you check the timeline. In reality, the place is full of bugs, leaking bathrooms, and poor hygiene. Nothing feels properly maintained, and management doesn’t seem to care. I’m genuinely glad I left. Before trusting the ratings, visit in person and see how poorly this property is kept.

Apartment: On 50
Review: This place is an absolute dump. The maintenance staff will just enter your residence without notice or warning. We had a severe mold problem in our apartment and the maintenance staff just painted over it each time it was reported. They constantly do giveaways in order to get people to leave a 5 star review. Find somewhere else to stay.

Apartment: On 50
Review: went to tour and even the example room they show was unacceptable, had dirty showers, and mold. misleading info and inattentive managers. good luck getting your emails responded to.

Apartment: On 50
Review: I’ve had a great experience at On50 so far. I love my apartment and the location is quiet, and so close to campus. While the apartment complex is on the older side, I think the maintenance and staff make the place shine and stand out amongst other student apartments . The maintenance is so quick to respond and very , especially Inoel. The staff is always so friendly and informative, especially Dewight and Emily.

Apartment: Station 42
Review: Great place, some of the best rates in walking distance to USF. Big rooms and kitchen/living room. Maintenance does a good job too and the leasing office staff are always friendly. No hidden fees un a lot of places around USF. Recommend

Apartment: Station 42
Review: I have lived here for 4 years now (it was Monticello back then) and I just renewed for another year. That shows how much I love this place! Although I really never had problems with the old management, the new management is organized and professional as to the way they handle things. Among all student housing within walking distance from campus, it has the cheapest rent while having the largest bedrooms. Office staff are very friendly and . Same with the maintenance staff, I've never had any problems with maintenance work, they were always attended to within 24 hours of the request.

Apartment: Station 42
Review: Overall my experience has been good so far the apartment is very spacious and has lots of storage space. However, our dryer hasn’t been working properly and we put in a request over and nothing has been resolved. Hopefully this changes… but otherwise definitely better than my old apartment

Apartment: Station 42
Review: I had an amazing experience thanks to Brandon! His customer service was top-notch from start to finish. He was friendly, knowledgeable, and went above and beyond to make sure everything was taken care of. Brandon made the whole process easy and stress-free, and I really appreciated his professionalism and attention to detail. If you’re looking for excellent service, ask for Brandon—you won’t be disappointed!

Apartment: Station 42
Review: Callie at the front desk of Station 42 is absolutely amazing. She is always welcoming, professional, and patient, and she goes out of her way to help residents with anything they need. From answering questions to handling packages and concerns, she does it all with kindness and efficiency. Her positive attitude makes the front desk feel warm and inviting, and she truly makes Station 42 feel home.

Apartment: Station 42
Review: Horrible un management. Will ignore you when you address problems and ask to email but never reply. Very deceiving and clearly don’t care about their customers and only money. I took over a lease and instead was placed in a random appartment with extremely filthy roomates. I was never told I was gonna be placed in a random appartment and management is extremely very un and unprofessional. Would not recommend living here at all!

Apartment: Station 42
Review: My 5 star review goes straight to Callie. She has been overtop for anything I needed for the entire year I lived at station. She helped me out a ton with my move in and move out process and even remembered my name which always made going to the front desk enjoyable

Apartment: Station 42
Review: I just toured Station 42, and it’s pretty amazing. It’s the equivalent of living on USF’s double room with a communal bathroom. It’s very cheap for an apartment compared to on-campus apartments (about $400 less). We had an amazing tour with Gabe, he was very informative. The 4x4 apartments are huge and come fully furnished. Super excited to live here next year!

Apartment: Station 42
Review: The staff are incredibly friendly and always willing to help, whether it’s a maintenance request or just answering questions. The apartment itself offers a lot space than I expected for student housing, with spacious rooms and areas. It’s been a comfortable and convenient place to live while focusing on my classes. Highly recommend for any student looking for a spacious living environment and great location!

Apartment: Station 42
Review: I lived here for 2 years. The room is spacious and the community is safe and clean. Maintenance was always fast and . I loved it very much and I had a nice time living here. The only thing is that they only have 4 bedroom floor plan, so living with a lot of people in a house might be annoying. If they have 1 or 2 bedroom would be better.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: Terrible experience. My lease started on Aug 23, and management confirmed in writing that I could move in on Aug 24. When I arrived after a 24-hour flight, the office was closed and no one gave me keys. I was left stranded with nowhere to stay. Completely unprofessional and unreliable. Don’t recommend!

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I’ve been a resident here for 2.5 years. Never really had a problem with anything, the staff is usually friendly and . The apartment itself is reasonable for the price. But, if you consider living here, read the lease very carefully. There was a vacant room in my female unit, and I was just informed by the office that a male would be moving in. I understand you can’t hold it against them because of discrimination policies, but this was a very unexpected move. It really is a business at the end of the day. Keep that in mind as you sign your lease.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I’ve had a great experience here with maintenance staff. They’re very kind and always do as much as they can until issues are resolved. Special shoutout to Ray, who recently had a task to get the a/c in my unit unclogged from some sort of buildup. I submitted a request in the evening and he was there early the next day. He took his time to clear the clogged line thoroughly and even cleaned the entire area before leaving. Professional, efficient, and respectful. I truly appreciated the care he put into the job. Excellent service!

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: Terrible apartment management. They are liars and completely irresponsible. They confirmed by email that I could move in on Aug 24, but when I arrived the office was closed and no keys were given. This shows how careless and unprofessional they are. Avoid this place at all costs.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: Do not live here man. We are constantly waiting on maintenance repairs. Bad service! It’s impossible to get a guest pass for parking. They tow anyone and everything else Ben if you live there. God forbid you need something from the front desk the leasing office is only open in prime working hours. So anyone who works a normal job will be AT WORK during the hours they are open. Therefore it’s a pain to get a package or to communicate a service issue.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I love to walk through the main entrance because it smells incredible and looks amazing with nicely decorated clubhouse. A very welcoming and comfortable experience with an easy to talk to staff. The rooms are well maintained with a spacious kitchen, living room and a bathroom for every occupant!!! The owners of this place did well along with the manager’s upkeep of the place!! It is a go-to place to send your student because of the location and all the amenities it has. I recommend it to everyone looking for a place to have peace of mind to send their child to stay while attending the nearby university.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I have been living at the flats since August of last year and I absolutely love it!! I love the floor plans and the how spacious the apartments are. I would definitely sign again if I weren't graduating! The location is right across from campus and has really good parking on site.The leasing office is also very accommodating. The price is reasonable for what you're getting and all of the amenities. I use the movie theater almost every week! I would definitely recommend living here!!

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: Great Location and Great Staff !! There are monthly club house events and a theatre to reserve for private use. The pool is huge and has grills for residents, and the fitness center is accessible 24/7. The Flats staff keeps this complex very clean and they are quick to respond to your needs/service request. I love The Flats !! :)

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I have had an absolutely amazing time living here! I haven't had any issues at all. My apartment is beautiful, and I've never had any problems with the staff. They have always been there to help when I need them. I'm going into my second year living here and I'm still just as excited about it as when I first moved in.

Apartment: The Flats at 4200
Review: I rented this apartment, but it's been really bad because the bedroom is next to a super noisy air conditioner. The noise is so loud that I can't sleep well. The people in charge didn't really care when I told them about the noise. It's expensive to live here, so it's not fair that I have to deal with this loud problem. I suggest not renting this place.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: Finding a new place to live has been super exciting! I'm stoked to tell you I snagged an awesome individual room that's comfy and won't break the bank. It's perfect if you want your own private spot without spending a ton. The area is really chill, great for focusing and unwinding after a crazy day.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: It’s not bad. The selling point is as always the price. Other than that, cleanliness, amount of community events, hand outs, maintenance response is really good. However, if I had to complain, I’d definitely do it on internet connection. It’s unstable at times (not frequent but not seldom too), my devices disconnect on their own, which doesn’t happen with any other wifi I connect to, so I guess it’s on the Wifi of the property, and the signal is sometimes kinda low even within the property. However, it doesn’t usually get in the way and starts working properly pretty soon. It’s just annoying to deal with.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: My daughter’s experience at The Ivy has been very upsetting and frustrating. She has been placed with a roommate who has frequent anger outbursts and has damaged my daughter’s personal property in the d kitchen. There is also constant dog hair all over the living room and kitchen, which creates an unsanitary and uncomfortable environment.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: I'm changing my review due to poor management. I have made multiple complaints about my roommate's boyfriend not being on the lease, yet he is living in our apartment. He smokes in the apartment, hogs the washing machine, and he is loud during the early hours of the morning. There has been nothing done to fix or resolve this problem. All they say is "We understand that d living can be challenging" and that if I have any further issues to notify the office immediately so they can review and take further action, but no action has been taken.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: Loved the tour. Helping my sister move in has been very stressful, but Mayawas incredibly and answered all of our questions very well. My sister is excited to move in here!

Apartment: The ivy
Review: I do not know why there are so many 1 star review but I personally as an international student as you can guess from my name. I think this place is good. I requested maintenance on Sunday. They fixed it perfectly on Thursday. over, the place is very good. My room is amazing. My roommate is wonderful, so I don't see why this place is not suitable for international students, especially if you're on a budget

Apartment: The ivy
Review: Dont take lease here if you are non white ... they dont care about your concerns and shows attitude based on your skin color. Definitely not recommending non whites or asians this place . You will regret moving in .

Apartment: The ivy
Review: Regretting signing a lease here. Broken furniture, roaches, appliances from the 1960 that don't work and make your bill higher, zero help with any management to replace anything, dog feces all over the property no one regulates picking up after pets the smell is horrendous. DO NOT let your child live here! There are just stealing your money.

Apartment: The ivy
Review: I have been living in this community for the past 2 years, and my experience has been wonderful. The management team is always responsive, friendly, and takes care of any concerns promptly. The maintenance service is excellent they are quick, professional, and reliable whenever any issue arises. The neighborhood is clean, peaceful, and very well-maintained, which makes it feel home. I truly appreciate the safe environment, the upkeep of the common areas, and the overall care that goes into this community. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a great place to live.”

Apartment: The ivy
Review: While the complex has many of the same problems as the complexes surrounding it, the quality is acceptable for the price. However, the customer service and office management make up for the difference, and the workers and maintenance team work efficiently, are cordial, and are time efficient.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: I’ve really enjoyed living at this apartment complex that Jeremy Zaifert helped me find. The whole process of getting the place was smooth, and Jeremy was super and easy to work with. He answered all my questions and made sure everything was straightforward, which made moving in a lot less stressful. The complex itself has been a great place to live. The apartments are nice, the area feels safe, and everything is well kept. One of my favorite parts is the pool area. It’s a great spot to relax after work or hang out with friends on the weekends. The pool is always clean and it’s a really chill place to spend time, especially when the weather is nice.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: The Venue at North Campus is a great living experience. The atmosphere is incredibly relaxing, making it a perfect place to hang out, get work done, or just unwind. The space is clean, well-maintained, and has a really welcoming vibe that makes you want to stay longer. One of the best parts of the experience is the staff. Jeremy, Matt, and Jake are all extremely and friendly. They go out of their way to make sure everything runs smoothly and that everyone feels comfortable. Their positive attitude and willingness to help really make a difference.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: Honestly, from the start my sister and I were very incredibly excited to move into the Venue. But after living here for 3-4 months, we’re looking forward to moving out. This place is advertised as luxurious, but when you really live here and can see the real circumstances, it’s not. We toured here before moving in, but all you get to see is the office/club house building. They have a mock one bedroom apartment with fake doors to represent the other bedrooms, and you don’t get to see the actual conditions of the place.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: I had lived at Venue for three years and recently moved out a couple months ago, and honestly, I stayed only stayed that long because it was cheap but now even a four-bedroom costs over a thousand dollars, which is ridiculous. Despite being a responsible resident keeping my unit clean and always paying rent on time I was treated every other resident dismissed at every turn having to go to the main office multiple occasions to even get little things fixed. The $40 collection notice I received once moving out was the final straw with them hence why I'm even bothering to leave a review or I would have just let it be. I had already provided evidence of preexisting damages and disgusting air quality in my vents, yet my emails went ignored for weeks, and I had to call repeatedly just to get a response.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: Even after speaking to staff, I was still forced to pay completely unprofessional and unethical.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: if you love the sounds of fire alarms, horrible dirty apartments, bugs, only 5 star ratings from staff workers, maintenance walking into your room without you approving them to at 7 AM, and an uncleaned apartment when you move in, then this is the place for you! oh also if you love over paying for such an “amazing” place as well who wouldn’t want to live in a 4x4 for over $1000 when you add in all of the random charges a month the give you!!😄

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: Lived here for a few years, was forced to stay in my car this summer for renovations but when I moved back in after they were done my apartment and room looked exactly the same. They sent people into the apartments when ever without notifying you before hand and lie about rent prices, they add about an extra 200$ onto your rent for “community fees” and have the time the plumbing is so backed up I don’t even showering in my own apartment, I’ve literally caught Covid from the staff 3 times and they just make excuses everytime I start asking questions because what they are doing here doesn’t seem legal or legitimate.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: This has been the worst management I have ever witnessed, they ignore you, brush off your maintenances requests, take months to get anything simple done, and don’t care about you. The townhomes are in terrible condition and they don’t care to fix the broken issues before you move in, again, they do not care about you. If you’re a student, it’s in your best interest to choose a place with better management and facilities.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: I recently signed a lease with Venue at North Campus and the process was very smooth. When I called all of the associates were very kind and answered all of my questions with confidence. I recommend checking out Venue at North Campus.

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: Good place to live. Very close to the university and in the road where most of the other apartments complexes are. I had some problems regarding about furniture in the past and it was quick for them to fix everything. Overall, good spot and recommend to anyone coming to USF! Staff is friendly and knowledgeable about the subjects!

Apartment: Venue at North Campus Campus
Review: I’ve had a great experience living at venue! Some of the new renovations could be done better however it is modernized and freshly painted. All the management experiences I have had have been wonderful and everyone is very timely and friendly with anything you may need. Also there are lots of giveaways and free events for residents.
